content: Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): In this scene, MJR, you can see a small square stone platform with small grass planted in between the small square sections. You are standing on the platform. On your left, there is a black wall that runs the entire length of the platform and on your right there is a large black building, with a wall rising halfway along the platform. Behind it, you can see the back of the building. You are facing slightly towards the upper left corner. The building has two rows of windows, the top row containing five windows with one in the center being very tall and having three small windows on either side of it. You can also see one window on the bottom row. There is only one lamp lit in the wall. Everyone else can see it. They all look impressed. The dark sky covers three quarters of the horizon. Some white tufts of clouds can be seen.**

Noster Eagle-Eye: Damn shame, ain't it? That big black monstrosity looming over us like some Daedra's arsehole. Used to be proud buildings in this city—stone carved by honest Nords, not this... whatever the fuck this is.

Jarl Elisif the Fair: Gods... even the stones of Solitude whisper of better days. Torygg used to walk these paths with me, speaking of restoring the city's grandeur. Now look at it—crumbling, like my husband's legacy. Noster Eagle-Eye: Hah! You think
Noster Eagle-Eye: What in Oblivion's rotten depths...

The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): As MJR stands in the dimly lit cobblestone alleyway under a starry night sky, the scene unfolds with ancient stone walls rising on either side, weathered and moss-covered, enclosing a narrow path that winds gently uphill. To the right, a sturdy wooden archway bridges the gap between two squat stone buildings, its timbers scarred and lantern-lit with flickering orange glows hanging from iron hooks, casting long shadows across the uneven ground. Beyond the arch, glimpses of more structures peek through—rooftops slanted with thatch and tile, and faint outlines of railings and fences amid patches of low greenery. A few evergreen shrubs dot the edges, their needles catching the starlight, while the path ahead continues its meandering course, bordered by low stone curbs and scattered wooden posts. The air feels crisp and still, with the distant silhouette of a tall, turreted tower piercing the dark horizon to the left.**

Serana: You know, beggar, in this peaceful night sky over Solitude, it's almost poetic—stars twinkling like the eyes of all the rich fucks up in the Blue Palace, pretending they don't see street rats like you starving below. Bet if Dibella ruled outright, she'd have you slurping from the noble teats by now, free love and all. Noster Eagle-Eye: Spare a septim for an old scout, miss? Fought in the Great War, lost this eye at Anvil, and now the night's too damn quiet—reminds me of the calm before those Thalmor bastards rained hell.

Noster Eagle-Eye: Ha, Dibella's graces? Lady, she'd sooner bless a Thalmor whore than toss a septim my way. These streets at night, with their fancy lanterns flickering like false hopes—reminds me of scouting Anvil under the stars, before the Dominion carved out my eye and left me rotting like yesterday's stew. Spare a coin for a vet who knows every shadow in Solitude hides more than moss?
Serana: Thalmor bastards, huh? peaceful Skyrim

Serana: Oh, Noster, you poor one-eyed Imperial fossil—shadows hiding moss and misery in Solitude's cozy alleys, huh? Under this starry blanket, with lanterns winking like Dibella's wet dreams across those thatched roofs, I'd share my last septim if it meant sticking it to the Thalmor pricks who left you eye-deep in shit. Ever scout a vampire nest?
